steps.title: Identify what would prove the concern unjustified
steps.content: The concern is that virtual tours would make in-person visits seem unnecessary — that tours would replace in-person attendance. To prove this concern unjustified, the museum needs evidence from in-person visitors showing that virtual tours don't suppress visitation. The strongest proof would be that virtual tours actually encourage in-person visits.

steps.title: Find the survey result that directly refutes the fear
steps.content: Choice A states that virtual tours convinced many in-person visitors to plan their visit — proving the opposite of what was feared. The other three either show tours weren't a factor (B), provide unrelated demographics (C), or suggest tours serve as a complement after visits (D) without proving they encourage attendance. That matches choice A — A is correct.
